title: वेङ्कटेश्वर सुप्रभातम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री वेङ्कटेश्वर सुप्रभातम् ॥

कौसल्या सुप्रजा राम पूर्वा सन्ध्या प्रवर्तते।
उत्तिष्ठ नरशार्दूल कर्तव्यं दैवमाह्निकम्॥ १॥

उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ गोविन्द उत्तिष्ठ गरुडध्वज।
उत्तिष्ठ कमलाकान्त त्रैलोक्यं मङ्गलं कुरु॥ २॥

मातस्समस्तजगतां मधुकैटभारेः
वक्षोविहारिणि मनोहरदिव्यमूर्ते।
श्रीस्वामिनि श्रितजनप्रिय दानशीले
श्रीवेङ्कटेशदयिते तव सुप्रभातम्॥ ३॥

तव सुप्रभातमरविन्दलोचने
भवतु प्रसन्नमुखचन्द्रमण्डले।
विधिशङ्करेन्द्रवनिताभिरर्चिते
वृषशैलनाथदयिते दयानिधे॥ ४॥

अत्र्यादि सप्तऋषयस्समुपास्य सन्ध्यां
आकाशसिन्धुकमलानि मनोहराणि।
आदाय पादयुगमर्चयितुं प्रपन्नाः
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम्॥ ५॥

पञ्चाननाब्जभवषण्मुखवासवाद्याः
त्रैविक्रमादिचरितं विबुधाः स्तुवन्ति।
भाषापतिः पठति वासरशुद्धिमारात्
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम्॥ ६॥

ईषत्प्रफुल्लसरसीरुहनारिकेल-
पूगद्रुमादिसुमनोहरपालिकानाम्।
आवातिमण्डपवनो ह्युपगन्तुमिच्छ-
त्यद्रेरधीश तव सुप्रभातम्॥ ७॥

उन्मीलयन्ति तव विश्वविलोचनेन
त्रैविक्रमं त्रिभुवनं त्रयिकेशवाद्याः।
दृक्पाङ्क्तिसेव्यचरणे तव सुप्रभातम्
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम्॥ ८॥

श्रीभूमिनायक दयादिगुणामृताब्धे
देवादिदेव जगदेकशरण्यमूर्ते।
श्रीमन्ननन्तगरुडादिभिरर्चिताङ्घ्रे
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम्॥ ९॥

श्रीपद्मनाभ पुरुषोत्तम वासुदेव
वैकुण्ठ माधव जनार्दन चक्रपाणे।
श्रीवत्सचिह्न शरणागतपारिजात
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम्॥ १०॥

कन्दर्पदर्पहर सुन्दर दिव्यमूर्ते
कान्ताकुचाम्बुरुहकुड्मलकोमलाङ्गे।
कालत्रयीकृतनमःसततं प्रभूत
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम्॥ ११॥

मीनाकृते कमठकोल नृसिंह वर्ण-
िन्श्रीराम नाम जलजायतलोचनाय।
एवं सदा जनतया तव सुप्रभातम्
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम्॥ १२॥

॥ इति श्री वेङ्कटेश्वर सुप्रभातम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Venkateswara Suprabhatam ||

Kausalya supraja Rama purva sandhya pravartate.
Uttishtha Narasharddula kartavyam daivam ahnikam. || 1 ||

Uttishtha-uttishtha Govinda uttishtha Garuda-dhvaja.
Uttishtha Kamala-kanta trailokyam mangalam kuru. || 2 ||

Matas-samasta-jagatam Madhu-Kaitabhareh
vaksho-viharini manohara-divya-murte.
Shri Svamini shrita-jana-priya dana-shile
Shri Venkatesha-dayite tava suprabhatam. || 3 ||

Tava suprabhatam-aravinda-lochane
bhavatu prasanna-mukha-chandra-mandale.
Vidhi-Shankara-Indra-vanitabhir-archite
Vrisha-shaila-natha-dayite dayanidhe. || 4 ||

Atryadi sapta-rishayah samupasya sandhyam
akasha-sindhu-kamalani manoharani.
Adaya pada-yugam-archayitum prapannah
Shri Venkatachala-pate tava suprabhatam. || 5 ||

Panchanana-abja-bhava-shanmukha-Vasavadyah
Traivikramadi-charitam vibudhah stuvanti.
Bhashapati pathati vasara-shuddhim-arat
Shri Venkatachala-pate tava suprabhatam. || 6 ||

Ishat-praphulla-sarasiru-ha-narikela-
puga-drumadi-sumanohara-palikanam.
Avati-mandapa-vano hyupagantum-ichchha-
tyadrer-adhisha tava suprabhatam. || 7 ||

Unmilayanti tava vishva-vilochanena
Traivikramam tribhuvanam trayi-Keshavadyah.
Drik-pankti-sevya-charane tava suprabhatam
Shri Venkatachala-pate tava suprabhatam. || 8 ||

Shri Bhumi-nayaka daya-digunamritabdhe
Devadi-deva jagad-eka-sharanya-murte.
Shriman-ananta-Garudadibhir-architangre
Shri Venkatachala-pate tava suprabhatam. || 9 ||

Shri Padmanabha Purushottama Vasudeva
Vaikuntha Madhava Janardana Chakra-pane.
Shrivatsa-chihna sharanagata-Parijata
Shri Venkatachala-pate tava suprabhatam. || 10 ||

Kandarpa-darpa-hara sundara divya-murte
kanta-kuchamburuha-kudmala-komalange.
Kala-trayi-krita-namah-satatam prabhuta
Shri Venkatachala-pate tava suprabhatam. || 11 ||

Minakrite Kamatha-Kola Nrisimha varn-
inShri Rama nama jalajayata-lochanaya.
Evam sada janataya tava suprabhatam
Shri Venkatachala-pate tava suprabhatam. || 12 ||

|| Iti Shri Venkateswara Suprabhatam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे कौसल्या के सुपुत्र राम! प्रातःकालीन सन्ध्या हो रही है। हे नरश्रेष्ठ! उठिए, दैवी कर्तव्य करना है॥ १॥

उठिए उठिए हे गोविन्द! उठिए हे गरुड ध्वज! उठिए हे कमलाकान्त! तीनों लोकों का मंगल कीजिए॥ २॥

हे समस्त जगत की माता! मधु-कैटभ शत्रु के वक्ष पर विहार करने वाली, मनोहर दिव्य मूर्ति, श्री स्वामिनी, शरणागतों को प्रिय, दानशील, श्री वेंकटेश की प्रिय! तुम्हारा सुप्रभात हो॥ ३॥

हे कमललोचने! प्रसन्न मुख चंद्रमंडल वाली, ब्रह्मा-शंकर-इन्द्र की पत्नियों द्वारा अर्चित, वृषशैलनाथ की प्रिय, हे दयानिधि! तुम्हारा सुप्रभात हो॥ ४॥

अत्रि आदि सप्त ऋषि सन्ध्या उपासना कर, आकाश गंगा के मनोहर कमल लेकर आपके चरणयुग्म की अर्चना करने आए हैं। हे श्री वेंकटाचलपति! आपका सुप्रभात हो॥ ५॥

पंचमुख शिव, ब्रह्मा, षण्मुख, इन्द्र आदि देवगण त्रिविक्रम आदि चरित का स्तवन कर रहे हैं। बृहस्पति दिन की शुद्धि का पाठ कर रहे हैं। हे वेंकटाचलपति! आपका सुप्रभात हो॥ ६॥

हे श्री पद्मनाभ! पुरुषोत्तम! वासुदेव! वैकुण्ठ! माधव! जनार्दन! चक्रपाणि! श्रीवत्स चिह्नधारी! शरणागतों के कल्पवृक्ष! हे श्री वेंकटाचलपति! आपका सुप्रभात हो॥ १०॥
benefits: Most auspicious morning prayer for divine awakening
Invokes the blessing of the complete blessings of lord venkateswara
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles and inauspiciousness from the day
Purifies the devotee's mind and environment
Traditionally associated with the grace of tirupati balaji into daily life
Traditionally invoked to establish positive energy for the entire day
description: Venkateswara Suprabhatam (वेङ्कटेश्वर सुप्रभातम्) is a sacred prayer dedicated to Lord Vishnu. This revered text originates from Prativadi Bhayankaram Annangaracharya. Regular recitation is believed to bestow the complete blessings of Lord Venkateswara, remove all obstacles and inauspiciousness from the day, and grant prosperity, peace, and success. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar), Saturday (Shanivar), and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Vaikuntha Ekadashi and Ekadashi. With 12 verses and a reading time of about 10 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Venkateswara Suprabhatam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Venkateswara Suprabhatam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Sacred morning prayer to awaken Lord Tirupati Balaji.
alsoKnownAs: Kausalya Suprajaa Rama Purvaa Sandhyaa Pravartate

seoFaqs.question: What is the Venkateswara Suprabhatam and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Venkateswara Suprabhatam (वेङ्कटेश्वर सुप्रभातम् — suprabhatam = good morning, awakening hymn) is the celebrated morning awakening prayer addressed to Lord Venkateswara (Balaji) of Tirumala-Tirupati, the most-visited Hindu pilgrimage shrine in the world. It was composed in the 15th century CE by Sri Prativadi Bhayankaram Annangaracharya (also known as PB Annan), a senior Sri Vaishnava acharya of the Vadakalai sub-school. The composition has four parts: Suprabhatam (29 awakening verses), Stotram (11 praise verses), Prapatti (16 surrender verses) and Mangalam (14 auspicious-conclusion verses), totalling 70 verses. It is recited every morning at 3:00 AM during the Suprabhata Seva at Tirumala — the first ritual of the day to awaken the Lord. (Sources: Sri Vaishnava tradition; Tirumala temple records; PB Annangaracharya works.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Kausalya Suprajaa Rama Purvaa Sandhyaa Pravartate?
seoFaqs.answer: This iconic opening verse of the Venkateswara Suprabhatam — Kausalya suprajaa Rama purvaa sandhyaa pravartate, uttishtha narashardula kartavyam daivam ahnikam — is one of the most recognised Sanskrit verses in modern India. It is in fact a verse from the Valmiki Ramayana (Bala Kanda 23.2), originally addressed by Vishvamitra to the young Rama. The translation: O Rama, the noble son of Kausalya, the eastern dawn (purva sandhya) is breaking; arise, O lion among men, the divine duties of the day must be performed. PB Annangaracharya brilliantly opened his Tirumala awakening hymn with this Ramayana verse, identifying Venkateswara with Rama and inviting the Lord to awaken at the eastern dawn at Tirumala. The verse is broadcast at 3 AM daily across India by All India Radio. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana 1.23.2; Venkateswara Suprabhatam.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the M. S. Subbulakshmi recording of the Suprabhatam so famous?
seoFaqs.answer: The Venkateswara Suprabhatam was rendered into a celebrated recording by Bharat Ratna M. S. Subbulakshmi in 1963 for HMV (His Master's Voice), produced under the guidance of T. K. Govinda Rao and the Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams (TTD). The recording — released as a 78 RPM gramophone record and subsequently as cassette and CD — became one of the bestselling Hindu devotional recordings of all time, with millions of copies sold and broadcast every dawn at 3:00 AM across India by All India Radio for decades. Her clear, devotional, classical-Karnatik rendition set the standard for all subsequent recordings of the Suprabhatam, and her voice has become inseparable from the awakening of Lord Balaji at Tirumala in the modern Hindu imagination. (Sources: HMV / Saregama records; TTD publications; M. S. Subbulakshmi biography.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the four parts of the Venkateswara Suprabhatam?
seoFaqs.answer: The complete Venkateswara Suprabhatam is structured in four sequential parts, each with its own theological purpose: (1) Suprabhatam (29 verses) — the awakening hymn, calling upon Venkateswara, his consorts, the temple gods, the rishis, the asuras, the devas, the cosmic elements and finally all devotees to participate in the Lord's awakening; (2) Sri Venkatesha Stotram (11 verses) — the praise hymn extolling the glory, beauty and grace of the Lord on the seven hills; (3) Sri Venkatesha Prapatti (16 verses) — the surrender hymn in classical Sri Vaishnava prapatti style declaring complete refuge in the Lord and his consort Sri Padmavati; (4) Sri Venkatesha Mangalam (14 verses) — the auspicious-conclusion hymn invoking mangalam (well-being) on the Lord, the consorts, the saints, the Acharyas and the devotees. (Sources: PB Annangaracharya Suprabhatam compilation.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how is the Venkateswara Suprabhatam recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Suprabhatam is recited every single morning at 3:00 AM at the Tirumala Sri Venkateswara temple as the Suprabhata Seva — the first paid temple seva of the day, where it is chanted by the temple Bhattars while the Lord is gently awakened from his cosmic sleep. Devotees can sponsor this seva and witness it. Outside Tirumala, the Suprabhatam is recited daily by Vaishnavas during the Brahma muhurta (3:30-5:30 AM) facing east, on every Saturday (Shanivar — sacred to Venkateswara since the legend that he descended to Tirumala on a Saturday in Kali Yuga), every Thursday (Guruvar — Vishnu day), Vaikuntha Ekadashi, Brahmotsavam at Tirumala (the great September-October festival), Dhanurmasa (the entire month of Margashirsha-Pushya, sacred to Vishnu), and as a daily morning awakening prayer in millions of Hindu homes. (Sources: TTD seva calendar; Sri Vaishnava paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits and special blessings does Suprabhatam recitation grant?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following benefits to regular Venkateswara Suprabhatam recitation: complete blessings of Lord Venkateswara as if one had performed pilgrimage to Tirumala, removal of all obstacles and inauspiciousness from morning onwards (suprabhata = good morning, ensuring an auspicious day), redemption of debts and financial liabilities (Lord Venkateswara famously took a loan from Kubera for his marriage to Padmavati, making him the cosmic resolver of debts), success in marriage and family harmony (Sri-Padmavati blessings), success in professional and material life through the Kali Yuga avatara of Vishnu, removal of Kali Yuga sins and afflictions, blessings for travel and pilgrimage especially to Tirumala, the supreme spiritual fruit of prapatti (complete surrender to the Lord), and ultimately Vaikuntha-prapti at the end of life. The phalashruti promises that the morning starts auspiciously for any home where Suprabhatam is sung. (Sources: Suprabhatam phalashruti; TTD literature.)
